name: Sauerbraten (Once More)
recipeIngredient: 1000 to 1500 g lean beef (preferably a rump piece)
-Literally nothing else needed for the marinade
100 g raisins, washed and soaked with water
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all marinade ingredients (water, cloves, vinegar, juniper berries, vinegar, onions, bay leaf, carrot, and black pepper) in a large bowl.
2. Place the beef in the marinade and ensure it is fully coated. Cover and refrigerate for 5 days (or longer in winter).
3. Remove the meat from the marinade and drain on paper towels.
4. In a large pot, cook the bacon in oil until crispy. Add the meat and fry all sides until browned.
5. Add remaining onions, peppers, and salt to the pot. Return some of the broth or additional marinade (if used).
6. Simmer for 1.5 hours, adding more broth or marinade as needed.
7. Stir flour into sour cream in a separate bowl until smooth.
8. Pour the sauce mixture into the pot with the meat and simmer for an additional 1 hour.
9. Add raisins and red current jelly, and continue to simmer for another hour.
10. Remove the meat from the pot and let cool on a platter for 10 minutes.
11. While the meat is cooling, stir flour into the sour cream sauce and cook until thickened, about 10 minutes.
12. Slice the cooled meat and place slices in the sauce.
13. Serve hot with sides like potatoes or bread.
Serving suggestions:
-1000 g will serve 7
-1500 g will serve 10
